heading: Establishment of the Fund
label: 290j Establishment of the Fund
notes: Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–164, title IX, § 90197 Stat. 1051Nov. 22, 1983“This title [enacting this subchapter] may be cited as the ‘United States-India Fund for Cultural, Educational, and Scientific Cooperation Act’.”

Ex. Ord. No. 12517. Delegation Concerning United States-India Fund for Cultural, Educational, and Scientific CooperationEx. Ord. No. 12517, , 50 F.R. 23105, provided:May 29, 1985By the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and statutes of the United States of America, including of the United States Code, and in order to delegate certain functions concerning the United States-India Fund for Cultural, Educational, and Scientific Cooperation to the Secretary of State, it is hereby ordered as follows:section 301 of Title 31. All functions vested in the President by the United States-India Fund for Cultural, Educational, and Scientific Cooperation Act (Title IX of , ; “the Act”) [ et seq.] are delegated to the Secretary of State.SectionPublic Law 98–16497 Stat. 105122 U.S.C. 290j. 2. India rupees provided to the President for purposes of Title IX of the Act and under Title III of the Departments of Commerce, Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriation Act, 1985 (, ) are allocated to the Secretary of the Treasury for investment to generate earnings for purposes of Title IX of the Act.SecPublic Law 98–41198 Stat. 1545Ronald Reagan.

children.heading: Agreement with Government of India; program purposes
children.label: a Agreement with Government of India; program purposes
children.text: The President is authorized to enter into an agreement with the Government of India for the establishment of a fund (hereafter in this subchapter referred to as the “Fund”) which would provide grants and other assistance for cultural, educational, and scientific programs of mutual interest. Such programs may include exchanges of persons, exchanges of information, and other programs of study, research, and scholarly cooperation. The agreement may also provide for the establishment of an endowment, a foundation, or other means to carry out the purposes of the agreement.

children.heading: United States representatives
children.label: b United States representatives
children.text: The United States representatives on any board or other entity created in accordance with the agreement to administer the Fund shall be designated by the President predominately from among representatives of United States Government agencies, including those administering programs which may be supported in whole or in part by the Fund.

children.heading: Funding of programs
children.label: c Funding of programs
children.text: United States Government agencies carrying out programs of the types specified in subsection (a) may receive amounts directly from the Fund for use in carrying out those programs.
